description:
You are helping a technical-writer who is reorganizing a collection of Markdown documentation stored under
    /home/user/docs/source/.

    1. Inside /home/user/docs/source/ you will find several “.md” files that already exist.  
       Every first-level section title in those files strictly follows the pattern  
       “# <Title text>” (hash, exactly one space, then the title text).

    2. Your job is to compile a deduplicated, alphabetically-sorted list of ALL first-level section titles
       extracted from every “.md” file in that directory and write that list to the new file  

          /home/user/docs/summary/HEADINGS.md  

       (create the /home/user/docs/summary/ directory if it does not yet exist).

    3. Output format for /home/user/docs/summary/HEADINGS.md (this is what the automated test will check):

       • Each heading must appear exactly once, even if it occurs in more than one source file.  
       • Sort headings in pure ASCII alphabetical order (i.e. the order produced by the `sort` utility with default
         locale settings).  
       • Write the list as a bullet list where every line begins with a hyphen + single space (“- ”), followed
         immediately by the heading text.  
       • Do not add any additional characters (no trailing spaces, no numbering, no extra blank lines).  
       • Ensure the file ends with a single trailing newline character.

       Example of correct formatting:

           - First
           - Second
           - Third
           <newline>

    4. Achieve the result using one single pipeline or compound shell command (use pipes, redirections, etc. as you
       see fit, but it must be executed as one logical command).  
       Do NOT manually edit the output file.

    5. After running your command, verify the contents of /home/user/docs/summary/HEADINGS.md in the terminal to
       ensure it exactly matches the required format.

    NOTE: You do not need elevated privileges; everything should be created inside the user’s home directory tree.
truth:
<!--
        Existing files before the agent starts
    -->
    <preexisting>
        <dir path="/home/user/docs/source/" />
        <file path="/home/user/docs/source/intro.md"><![CDATA[
# Introduction
This project contains example Markdown documentation.

Some more prose that is irrelevant for this task.
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/docs/source/setup.md"><![CDATA[
# Setup
Follow these steps to prepare your environment.

# Installation
Run the installation script after cloning the repository.
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/docs/source/usage.md"><![CDATA[
# Usage
After installation, you can run the program as follows:
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/docs/source/duplicateHeading.md"><![CDATA[
# Introduction
This is a duplicate heading introduced on purpose.
]]></file>
    </preexisting>

    <!--
        File that MUST exist after the agent completes the task
    -->
    <postcondition>
        <dir path="/home/user/docs/summary/" />
        <file path="/home/user/docs/summary/HEADINGS.md"><![CDATA[
- Installation
- Introduction
- Setup
- Usage
]]></file>
    </postcondition>
